_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Ability to monitor Active Directory (AD) for creation of unauthorized/potentially malicious accounts
reference_document_element_description: Implement AI application security controls, including prompt injection defenses, output filtering, access controls, and audit logging.
comments: DCWF ksa intersects with WIDAI SP domain. SP-T-001 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: AI-T-017 (AI, cosine: 0.306), RC-T-008 (RC, cosine: 0.295).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark.
primary_method.significance: STS 4.5/10 (raw 0.448). No substantive shared vocabulary — relationship is purely semantic. DCWF-only terms [ability, accounts, active, ad, creation, directory] vs WIDAI-only terms [access, ai, application, audit, controls, defenses] define the semantic distance between the descriptions.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.847 (P=0.839, R=0.854). Balanced P/R — the 12-word DCWF element and 16-word WIDAI KSA cover each other's token-level semantic content symmetrically.

nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: contradiction-dominant (99.6%). Despite 'Intersects with' classification, the model detects semantic tension between "Ability to monitor Active Directory (AD) for creation of unauthorized/potentiall" and "Implement AI application security controls, including prompt injection defenses,". Indicates these concepts operate in adjacent but distinct professional domains — shared vocabulary, different application context.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.365 < STS 0.448 (gap -0.083). Cross-attention finds deeper alignment than independent encoding. The relationship depends on cross-text reasoning — concept-level mapping between [ability, accounts, active] and [access, ai, application] that bi-encoders structurally cannot capture.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 0.0%. Zero substantive shared tokens. The entire relationship is semantic — no lexical trace. DCWF terms: [ability, accounts, active, ad, creation, directory]. WIDAI terms: [access, ai, application, audit, controls, defenses].
